summary: Russian forces have deployed the "Yolka" automated drone interceptor, a 1.3 kg system capable of reaching 230 km/h and using AI for autonomous target engagement. Ukraine launched a large-scale drone attack on March 29, with Russian air defenses claiming to have shot down at least 203 UAVs, including strikes on the Ust-Luga port. Russian operators from the Rubikon Center reported destroying 136 Ukrainian targets on March 28, including personnel and fixed-wing UAVs. Ukrainian forces survived an FPV drone attack in the Kostiantinivka area, with soldiers using drone detectors to anticipate the strike. Russian Ka-52 helicopter crew were eliminated after a crash landing near Nadiivka, Donetsk, when additional drones struck during their evacuation attempt.
